# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_l4l7_device_selection_interface_context (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `acl` (Boolean) Specifies whether an Access Control List (ACL) is applied to the logical interface. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `bridge_domain` (String) The Bridge Domain to bind to the Context.
- `bridge_domain_tenant` (String) The tenant the Bridge Domain resides in. Omit this variable if both context and Bridge Domain are in the same tenant. Intended use case is for when the Bridge Domain is in the common tenant, but the context is not.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `context` (String) The name of the logical interface context.
- `contract` (String) The name of an existing contract.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) A brief description for the Logical Interface Context.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `external_epg` (String) The name of the External Network Instance Profile (External EPG or ExtEpg) associated with the L3Out.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `graph` (String) The name of an existing Service Graph Template.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `l3_destination` (Boolean) Whether the context is a Layer3 destination. The APIC defaults to `true` when unset during creation.
- `l3out` (String) The name of the Layer 3 Outside (L3Out) object.
- `l3out_tenant` (String) The tenant in which the L3Out resides. Omit this variable if both context and L3Out are in the same tenant.
- `logical_device` (String) The Logical Device to bind the context to.
- `logical_interface` (String) The Logical Interface to bind the context to.
- `node` (String) The name of an existing Service Graph Node.
- `permit_handoff` (Boolean) Indicates whether to allow handoff of traffic to the associated logical interface. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `permit_log` (Boolean) Whether to log permitted traffic. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `redirect_policy` (String) The Redirect Policy to bind the context to.
- `redistribute` (String) A list of routing protocols whose routes should be redistributed.
- `rule_type` (Boolean) Indicates whether the context uses a specific rule type for traffic handling. The APIC defaults to `false` when unset during creation.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tenant` (String) The name of an existing tenant.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
